The Stable R0 subset is closed, pure, deterministic, and total: normalized values/literals/StaticInt arithmetic/comparison/word Boolean operators only; no I/O, provider, mutation, suspension, reflection, allocation with observable identity, solver search, or thrown failure. Checks occur only at explicit construction/cast/argument/return/pattern boundaries.

Overload resolution prefers exact fixed-arity matches over repeated positional rest, and repeated positional over named rest. Remaining ties are errors; source order is not a tie-breaker.

`Result<T, error E>` is a value channel and `throws E` is a control-transfer channel. ErrorSet normalizes as a duplicate-free unordered type set for equality and subset checks while preserving source order for diagnostics. One operation may not expose the same recoverable error family through both Result and throws.
The same recoverable error family must not be exposed simultaneously in Result<T,error E> and throws E for the same operation.
Plain-value reuse and explicit Shared<T> handle sharing are separate. Owners never acquire shared identity or cross-task permission by implicit conversion.

Expr -> $x[: T] and Expr -> $$x[: T] are CST-preserved statement-only sugar for let x[: T] = Expr and var x[: T] = Expr. They normalize before semantic checking and leave no distinct AST/HIR/MIR/xVM/LLVM operation. The target is one fresh identifier; chaining, patterns, places, members, indexes and guard attachment are rejected.
Every root new calls exactly one super constructor on every successful path; : super!() is implied only when accessible.

display_name: Schema construction authority and field visibility law
notes: A Type${...} expression requires visible schema authority for the target type and cannot write private/inaccessible fields or skip required schema entries.
Every schema supplies a construction label row and a scope-sensitive projection label row; both are non-forgeable checker descriptors.

display_name: Schema construction public API projection law
notes: Public API digest records Type${...} as schema construction and preserves its field-label residue distinct from constructors.
`**schemaValue` projects statically known visible labels into a materialization or named-argument channel; Map is not a projection source.
#scoped denotes a nonescaping callable bounded by the receiving invocation region. Storage, return, continuation retention, task/actor transfer, and crossing the region are rejected.

display_name: Stable law for scoped extension activation boundaries
notes: Stable boundary: extension activation is lexical and cannot be resolved by import order.

Stable scoped-import statement pushes a compile-time name environment only for its nested block and pops it on every exit; it is not an expression or runtime activation object. Product execution NOT_RUN.
Stable family grouping block-prologue imports and nested scoped-import statements; neither route performs runtime loading or extension activation.
Comma-separated specs create one compile-time lexical frame for the following `in` block; import and use semantics remain distinct. Product execution remains NOT_RUN.
`use path { ... }` is a statement-only lexical activation frame. Nested depth is never a candidate priority.
